title: Hosted and Self-Hosted Runners, Isolation, Capacity, and Compatibility · Independent Delivery Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer hosted and self-hosted runners, isolation, capacity, and compatibility into a new runner isolation and capacity policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of runner isolation and capacity policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.
Threat-model persistence, workspace residue, credentials, network reachability, daemon sockets, labels, groups, and repository eligibility for self-hosted runners.
Design single-job ephemeral runner registration, identity, network segmentation, secret delivery, teardown, log retention, and orphan cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Hosted Runner Image Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 48 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Pin or record runner OS, architecture, installed software, action runtime, image release, and migration assumptions instead of trusting latest as immutable.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24-5abbj1". Tailor its runner-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24 into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, keep Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24 connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24-5abbj1
  - competency: ci-runner-compatibility-action-node24
    artifact: runner-policy.yaml
    decision: runner compatibility action node24
    environment: sandbox Google Cloud project with a private Cloud Run service
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: 5abbj1
    change: |
      runner-image: ubuntu-24.04-reviewed
      action-runtime: node24
      minimum-runner: 2.327.1-or-later
      untrusted-work: github-hosted-ephemeral
      self-hosted: trusted-release-only
      network: segmented
      cleanup: single-job-destroy
      capacity: queue-budget-and-max-parallel
    verify: inspect runner version image release labels groups network authority queue duration and teardown evidence
    failure: Reject latest as immutable, reused public-PR runners, workspace cleanup as host destruction, or unbounded parallelism.
    repair: Record compatibility, isolate untrusted work, destroy ephemeral capacity, segment authority, and measure queue and cost.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify runner version compatibility for Node 24-based actions and plan staged upgrades, rollback, and GHES differences.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24 precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab public pull request reaches a persistent self-hosted runner with Docker authority and cloud credentials while 40 jobs queue. Bound eligibility, ephemerality, compatibility, capacity, evidence, and destruction. Competency probe: Verify runner version compatibility for Node 24-based actions and plan staged upgrades, rollback, and GHES differences.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Runner Compatibility Action Node24, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Updating an action major version cannot require a newer runner service.
